Title: Role of LEAFLESS, an AP2/ERF family transcription factor, in the regulation of trichome specialized metabolism

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family; single AP2 DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against biotic and abiotic stresses
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf/stem glandular trichomes, especially type I/IV trichome tip cells on aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates acylsugar biosynthesis and also promotes accumulation of a trichome-associated flavonoid; VIGS reduces total acylsugars by about 65% and decreases dimethyl kaempferol
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpLFS showed very strong trichome-enriched expression (~208-fold) in S. pennellii, comparable to ASAT genes. 2. VIGS of SpLFS markedly reduced total acylsugars and major acyl chains, and also reduced a methylated flavonol. 3. RNA-seq of SpLFS-VIGS plants showed downregulation of many known/candidate acylsugar and flavonoid metabolic genes. 4. Co-expression analysis placed SpLFS with acylsugar/flavonoid genes in a strongly correlated module. 5. EMSA demonstrated direct binding of SpLFS to GCC box or GCC-like motifs in promoters of acylsugar/flavonoid genes, supporting direct transcriptional regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of trichome specialized metabolites; transcriptional control of acylsugar and flavonoid pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Auxin-inducible relationship suggested for close tomato homologs; not established for SpLFS in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SpLFS also affects trichome development, but acylsugar reduction is much stronger than trichome density reduction, indicating acylsugar biosynthesis and trichome development are related but distinct outputs. Expression is predominantly in tip cells of digitate glandular trichomes (type I/IV).
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS); LC-MS metabolite profiling; GC-MS acyl chain analysis; RNA-seq differential expression; WGCNA co-expression; promoter EMSA; promoter::GFP transcriptional reporter; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively regulates many acylsugar and flavonoid metabolic genes; VIGS caused downregulation of ASATs, fatty acid synthase-related genes, acyl-activating enzyme candidates, RuBisCO small subunit, ABC transporter candidates, IPMS3, and flavonoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Sopen02g027670 (acyl-activating enzyme); Sopen09g030520 (ACYLSUGAR ACYLHYDROLASE 3); Sopen06g034830 (MYRICETIN O-METHYLTRANSFERASE 1); Sopen05g009610 (SpKAR1); Sopen08g005140 (SpIPMS3); Sopen11g026960 (SpASAT3); additional acylsugar/flavonoid genes with GCC/GCC-like motifs in promoters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering or breeding enhanced acylsugar-based defense in solanaceous crops; could be used to elevate protective acylsugars, but developmental effects on trichomes and leaf initiation should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown at 24°C:20°C day:night, 16-h photoperiod; imazapyr treatment is referenced from prior data as a condition associated with repression of SpLFS and acylsugar genes
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native SpLFS promoter (~1832 bp) used for transcriptional reporter; VIGS via TRV system
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding to GCC box/GCC-like cis-elements
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Critical positive regulator of trichome acylsugar biosynthesis; stronger association with acylsugar levels than with trichome density
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly trichome-enriched (~208-fold); expressed in seedlings and trichome-bearing aerial tissues; barely expressed in roots; promoter activity predominantly in tip cells of type I/IV glandular trichomes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly trichome-enriched; strongest in type I/IV glandular trichome tip cells of aerial tissues

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against biotic and abiotic stresses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VIGS targeting these two orthologs reduced acylsugar production by about 15% overall, with 3–30% reduction in individual acylsugars
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two N. benthamiana SpLFS orthologs in the 'upper' clade were selected for functional testing. 2. Simultaneous VIGS of both genes reduced acylsugar accumulation. 3. Results support a conserved role of LFS orthologs in acylsugar biosynthesis across Solanaceae.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Effect size was smaller than in S. pennellii, possibly because of incomplete silencing or redundancy from a third homolog (Niben101Scf03045g14001).
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: virus-induced gene silencing (VIGS); LC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential conserved regulators for tuning acylsugar-based defense in Nicotiana and related Solanaceae, though redundancy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown under controlled conditions as described for growth assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive transcriptional regulation inferred from orthology and VIGS phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulators contributing to acylsugar accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All three close homologs showed similar RNA-seq coverage in available expression data; selected genes belong to trichome-enriched upper clade by comparative analysis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative RNA-seq coverage from Bombarely et al. 2012; no accession provided in this paper
